description: A child eating soup and a deer cropping leaves both 'nuola'. The verb covers taking food into the body through the mouth as an eating event. 'lumera' (nourish) concerns what sustains growth or health, which a meal may or may not accomplish.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and continue into rounded high-back 'u'. Give stressed middle-height back rounded 'o' its own syllable. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l', let air pass along its sides, and finish with central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: 'nuola' shares its opening 'nu.o' with 'nuora' (food), while lateral 'l' turns the final syllable away from food's tap. The audible kinship makes eating and food easy to remember as neighbours without making either form a derivation.
usage_notes: Food normally stands before 'nuola', though context can supply it. A meal may include soup or milk, so eating and drinking can describe the same occasion from different angles. The event noun supplies an act or period of eating rather than the food itself.

semantic_domains.activity: taking food into the body as eating
